pi_items.source: UIUC Airfoil Data Site (m-selig.ae.illinois.edu); Airfoil Tools (airfoiltools.com); Direct inspection of benchmarks/cua_world/environments/qblade_env/data/airfoils/naca6412.dat which contains 'NACA 6412' header and standard coordinate pairs

Airfoil Tools XFoil analysis database (http://airfoiltools.com/polar/details?polar=xf-naca6412-il-500000); Published research on NACA 6412 shows analysis at Re=4.52×10⁵; Standard for wind turbine blade design

Task description specifies -10 degrees as minimum angle of attack; QBlade forum discussions confirm typical XFoil ranges include -10 to +25 degrees for wind turbine airfoil analysis; literature shows XFoil can handle up to 28.5 degrees

Task description specifies 25 degrees as maximum angle of attack; QBlade documentation and forum discussions confirm 360-degree extrapolation requires base polar data that can extend past stall; XFoil literature confirms capability up to ~28.5 degrees with decreasing accuracy near stall
Task description and export_result.sh script confirm this filename; consistent with QBlade naming conventions for 360-degree extrapolated polars
Task description, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh all specify this directory; env.json confirms user 'ga' exists with home directory at /home/ga

privileged_info_summary: The NACA 6412 is a real, standardized airfoil from the NACA airfoil database with publicly available coordinate data. XFoil analysis at Reynolds number 500,000 is a standard condition documented in the Airfoil Tools database and published research. The angle of attack range from -10° to 25° is a typical specification for wind turbine blade XFoil analysis, covering attached flow up to and slightly beyond stall regions. QBlade's 360-degree polar extrapolation using the Viterna/Montgomerie methods is a documented and real workflow for extending limited XFoil polar data to the full 360° angle of attack range required by Blade Element Momentum (BEM) turbine simulations. The output file naca6412_360polar.txt and directory /home/ga/Documents/airfoils are consistent with QBlade conventions.
